name: Upper Azuer River Trail
name_original: Molino de el Rezuelo por la Ermita Virgen del Espino — circular desde Membrilla
description: Hike the Molino de el Rezuelo por la Ermita Virgen del Espino, an easy 4.7-mile circular route exploring historical mills and a hermitage ne
region_intro: Hiking around Membrilla reveals a landscape characterized by extensive agricultural plains, particularly vineyards, and the presence of the Azuer River. The terrain generally features gentle gradients, making many routes accessible. The region's trails often pass through rural areas, offering views of traditional La Mancha features. Best hiking trails around Membrilla The most popular hiking route is From Manzanares to Membrilla — circular by the tinajas of La Mancha, a 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 17 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route explores the local agricultural landscape and traditional tinajas. Another top favourite among local hikers is The vineyards and the ancient baths of El Peral — circular, a difficult 15.2 miles (24.5 km) path. This trail traverses vineyards and includes historical points of interest like ancient baths. Local hikers also love the Upper Azuer River Trail, a 4.8 miles (7.6 km) trail leading through river plains, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Hiking around Membrilla is defined by vineyard landscapes, river paths, and agricultural plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging routes through rural areas. The routes in Membrilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Membrilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Membrilla?
A: Membrilla offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with nearly 100 trails documented on komoot. These range from easy strolls to more challenging routes, ensuring options for all fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Membrilla?
A: The terrain around Membrilla is primarily characterized by extensive agricultural plains, particularly vineyards, and the presence of the Azuer River. You'll find generally gentle gradients, making many routes accessible, often passing through rural areas with views of traditional La Mancha features.

Q: Are there easy hiking options for beginners or casual walkers in Membrilla?
A: Yes, Membrilla is very beginner-friendly. Almost half of the trails, around 50 routes, are classified as easy. An excellent option for a relaxed walk is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201337374/upper-azuer-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Upper Azuer River Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.8 miles (7.6 km) and follows the river plains.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Membrilla?
A: Yes, many routes in Membrilla are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular circular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201362850/from-manzanares-to-membrilla-circular-by-the-tinajas-of-la-mancha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Manzanares to Membrilla — circular by the tinajas of La Mancha&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the local agricultural landscape and traditional tinajas.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Membrilla suitable for families with children?
A: Given the generally gentle gradients and numerous easy routes, many trails in Membrilla are suitable for families. The flat agricultural paths and riverside walks provide safe and enjoyable environments for children to explore.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Membrilla?
A: Most trails in rural areas around Membrilla are generally dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural land or near livestock. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet, as shade can be limited.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Membrilla?
A: While hiking, you'll encounter the agricultural landscape dominated by vineyards and the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3451633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo del Peral&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199969050/the-vineyards-and-the-ancient-baths-of-el-peral-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The vineyards and the ancient baths of El Peral — circular&lt;/a&gt;, also include historical points of interest such as ancient baths.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Membrilla?
A: Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are ideal for hiking in Membrilla, offering pleasant temperatures and often clear skies. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters are generally mild but can be chilly.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Membrilla, especially in spring?
A: In spring, layered clothing is recommended as temperatures can vary. Lightweight, breathable fabrics are best. Comfortable hiking shoes are essential, and a hat and sunscreen are advisable due to the open nature of the landscape. Always carry enough water.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Membrilla?
A: The trails in Membrilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 45 reviews. Hikers often praise the peaceful rural atmosphere, the extensive vineyard views, and the accessibility of many routes for a relaxing outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any less-known trails worth exploring for a unique experience?
A: For a route that combines history with nature, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39837223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Antón – Our Lady of Altagracia loop from Manzanares&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate trail offers a glimpse into local heritage while traversing the serene landscape.

Q: Can I find places for a pub lunch or refreshments along the hiking routes?
A: While many routes pass through rural areas, some may lead near or through small villages where you can find local establishments for refreshments or a meal. It's always a good idea to check the route details on komoot beforehand or carry your own provisions, especially on longer trails.
tips: Previously it was a chapel of the defunct Castillo del Tocón from the 11th century. The first building of the hermitage of the Virgen del Espino received at that time the name of "Santa María del Castillo" or "Nuestra Señora del Castillo" and, it was the chapel of the fortress of the Tocón, the only construction that remained standing. the entire military ensemble.
Reconstructed mill, including its original machinery, which allows a better understanding of the most recent past of milling.

From the 15th century, of which its three covers stand out, in Gothic, flamboyant and other Baroque styles. Building with 60 m. long by 16 m. Wide. Its floor plan is of a Latin cross with a short transept and various chapels on its sides; the apse is hexagonal, with a central niche, and at the foot of the temple there is a raised choir on two carpal arches.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Molino de el Rezuelo por la Ermita Virgen del Espino — circular desde Membrilla trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as easy, making it suitable for a wide range of hikers, including beginners and families. It features minimal elevation gain, ensuring a comfortable walk.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers varied terrain. You'll walk on approximately 5.56 km of designated paths, 1.17 km of streets, and 871 meters of paved road. Additionally, there are about 3.00 km of unpaved track, 977 meters of paved track, and 871 meters of asphalt sections.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail connects several significant landmarks. You'll encounter the historic El Rezuelo Mill, an imposing restored building that now functions as a museum showcasing traditional grain-milling processes. Another key highlight is the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Hawthorn, a sanctuary built on a 'motilla' within the remnants of the ancient El Tocón castle. The Church of Saint James the Greater is also nearby.

faqs.question: Is the Molino de el Rezuelo por la Ermita Virgen del Espino — circular desde Membrilla trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas, near livestock, or on sections shared with roads.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring is an excellent time to visit, as the landscape comes alive with a vibrant 'explosion of colors' from blooming vegetation. The traditional rural Manchego scenery, enhanced by the Azuer river and its historic water mills, is beautiful year-round, but spring offers a particularly picturesque experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, as a circular route starting from Membrilla, you can typically find parking within the town or near the initial access points to the trail. Look for public parking areas in Membrilla to begin your hike.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Molino de el Rezuelo por la Ermita Virgen del Espino — circular desde Membrilla trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with other paths. Approximately 40% of the trail follows the 'Camino de arriba el río', 6% is part of the 'Camino de San Carlos del Valle', and 5% aligns with the 'Camino de Alcázar'.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect to see traditional rural Manchego landscapes. The route winds through an area historically rich with water mills along the Azuer river, offering a blend of natural beauty and historical heritage. In spring, the scenery is particularly vibrant with blooming vegetation.

faqs.question: Is the trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Molino de el Rezuelo por la Ermita Virgen del Espino — circular desde Membrilla is a circular route, meaning you will end your hike at the same point where you started without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: As a rural trail, dedicated facilities like restrooms or official water sources are not typically found directly on the path. It's advisable to use facilities in Membrilla before starting and carry sufficient water for your hike.
